mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with a declining federal funds rate creates a dual expansionary impulse that boosts consumer spending and lowers financing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2 percent and FEDFUNDS falls, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and rising business investment, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4 percent, or the signal reverses direction in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects robust economic activity and expanding corporate revenue bases, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 200, elevated inflation erodes real consumer purchasing power and forces monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in greater than 70 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL surpasses 200, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals synchronized strength in the labor market and construction sector, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and HOUST growth signal yields negative or flat forward returns, or the interaction term loses predictive significance in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment indicates a contracting economic cycle that suppresses corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month during recessionary transitions.
failure_condition: The dual deterioration signal fails to predict negative returns when the economy is in a confirmed expansion, or the mechanism shows positive predictive power in late-cycle phases.
